subject: English
chapter: The Tale of Custard the Dragon
title: The Tale of Custard the Dragon – Easy Short Notes

key_points: The poem is written by Ogden Nash
Belinda lives in a little white house with her pets
Her pets are Ink the kitten, Blink the mouse, Mustard the dog, and Custard the dragon
Custard is mocked for being cowardly because he cries for a safe cage
When a pirate attacks, Custard bravely fights and defeats him
characters: Belinda – A little girl who lives with her pets
Custard – A dragon who appears cowardly but proves brave
The Pirate – A threatening intruder who enters Belinda’s house
themes: True courage is shown through actions, not words
Boasting does not mean real bravery
literary_devices: Simile – Used frequently, such as 'as brave as a tiger in a rage'
Irony – Custard is called cowardly but turns out to be the bravest
Humour – The poem uses exaggeration and funny situations
exam_tips: Remember that Custard is initially mocked but becomes the hero
Focus on the theme that actions prove real bravery
Mention the pirate episode as the climax of the poem
Use examples of similes when discussing poetic devices
